tasks.title: Create career-data.json with full career history
tasks.description: Create content/career-data.json containing all career nodes (roles, companies, skills, projects, education) and edges extracted from the 3 resume PDFs. Include ~75-80 nodes and all relationship edges. Skills should have proficiency ratings 1-5. Each node needs id, type, label, and relevant metadata (dates, descriptions, categories, URLs).

verification.command: node -e "const d=require('./content/career-data.json'); console.log('Nodes:', d.nodes.length, 'Edges:', d.edges.length); if(d.nodes.length<60) process.exit(1);"

tasks.context:
## Spec Context
Career data from resumes:

Roles:
1. Senior Linux Cloud Engineer — Smith Micro Software (Jan 2022 – Oct 2025)
2. Principal Consultant — Norton Consulting (Jan 2017 – Present)
3. Senior Full Stack Developer / Senior Technology Specialist — Ronin Advertising (Aug 2012 – Dec 2016)
4. SEO Manager — Higher Images (Dec 2006 – Jan 2012)

Education:
1. BS Information Sciences — Robert Morris University (Aug 2002 – Dec 2003)
2. Associates in Networking & Programming — CCAC (Jan 2001 – Jun 2002)

Skills (~60 unique, 9 categories from site.json):
Cloud & Infrastructure: AWS, GCP, Azure, IBM Cloud, EKS, EC2, IAM, Route53, CloudFormation, Global Accelerator
DevOps & IaC: Terraform, Ansible, Packer, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitOps, Zabbix, Jira, Prometheus, Grafana
Linux & Platforms: Debian, Ubuntu, CentOS, RHEL, Nginx, Apache, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Redis, PHP
Containers & Orchestration: Docker, Kubernetes, OpenShift, K9s, kubectl, Helm, ETCD, LXC, ProxMox
Security & Compliance: PCI Compliance, GHAS, Trivy, Wazuh, Dependabot, Patch Management, Cyber Security
AI/ML & Data: TensorFlow, PyTorch, Jupyter, MLOps, Data Pipelines, LLMs
Coding & Scripting: Python, Bash, Go, JavaScript, TypeScript, PHP, C#, Rust, Java, Node.js
3D Printing & Design: FDM, Resin, Tinkercad, Klipper, Prototyping
Networking: DNS, TCP/IP, VPNs, Tailscale, pfSense, Load Balancing, BGP, VLAN

Projects: skitz0prints, ZenDeploy, mcp-discord-notify, GMMK2-96, MD-easy, neurOs

Edge types: worked-at (role→company), used-skill (role→skill), built-with (project→skill), earned-at (education→school), part-of (skill→category)

Proficiency scale: 5=expert (10+ years daily use), 4=advanced, 3=proficient, 2=working knowledge, 1=familiar

GitHub language history for proficiency dating:
- Shell: since 2014
- JavaScript: since 2016
- Python: since 2017
- TypeScript: since 2020
- Go: since 2022
- Rust: since 2023
- C: since 2023

## Data Schema
```json
{
  "nodes": [
    { "id": "string", "type": "role|company|skill|project|education", "label": "string", "category": "string?", "startDate": "ISO?", "endDate": "ISO|present?", "proficiency": "1-5?", "description": "string?", "url": "string?" }
  ],
  "edges": [
    { "source": "nodeId", "target": "nodeId", "type": "worked-at|used-skill|built-with|earned-at|part-of" }
  ]
}
```

IMPORTANT: The word 'blog' must NEVER appear anywhere. No Co-Authored-By in commits.

tasks.title: Install sigma.js, graphology, and forceatlas2 dependencies
tasks.description: Install the 3 required npm packages: sigma, graphology, and graphology-layout-forceatlas2. Run npm install and verify they appear in package.json dependencies.

verification.command: node -e "require('sigma'); require('graphology'); require('graphology-layout-forceatlas2'); console.log('All deps OK');"

tasks.context:
## Spec Context
NFR-1: Dependencies — sigma.js, graphology, graphology-layout-forceatlas2 (3 new deps)
NFR-2: Bundle size — sigma (~50KB gzip), graphology (~15KB gzip), forceatlas2 (~5KB gzip)

Run: npm install sigma graphology graphology-layout-forceatlas2

These are imported client-side via Astro's <script> tag processing (AC-3).

IMPORTANT: No Co-Authored-By in commits.

tasks.title: Add CareerData TypeScript interfaces to site.ts
tasks.description: Add CareerNode, CareerEdge, and CareerData interfaces to src/data/site.ts. Add a getCareerData() function that imports and returns content/career-data.json. Also add CareerData to the SiteData interface as an optional field, or keep it as a separate import — design decision: separate import is cleaner since career-data.json is a new file not part of site.json.

tasks.title: Create KnowledgeGraph.astro component
tasks.description:
Create the main KnowledgeGraph.astro component as a single-file Astro component. This is the largest task. Component must include:

**Frontmatter**: Import types, accept Props (skills: SkillCategory[], careerData: CareerData, revealIndex?).

**HTML Template**:
- Section with id='skills' (retains goto compatibility)
- Fallback skills grid (from Skills.astro markup, shown before JS)
- Graph container (hidden until JS: canvas, tooltip, controls, legend)
- SR-only accessible text list of all skills and connections

**CSS (scoped)**:
- All colors from CSS custom properties (--palette-N, --accent, --bg-*, --fg-*, --border)
- Responsive: 320px+ viewport
- Tooltip styles, legend, controls
- .cmd-highlight/.cmd-dim support for filter integration

**Client-side Script**:
- Import sigma, graphology, forceatlas2
- Parse career data from data attribute
- Build graph: add nodes with type-based sizes/colors, add edges
- Compute ForceAtlas2 layout (batch, seeded)
- Animated entrance (staggered by type, 1.5s total, prefers-reduced-motion check)
- Click handler: highlight connected, dim others
- Click background: reset
- Hover: show/position tooltip
- Drag: Sigma built-in
- Zoom controls: buttons + wheel/pinch
- Theme change: MutationObserver on data-theme, re-read CSS vars, update colors
- Expose window.__graphFilter(keyword) for CommandBar integration
- Hide fallback, show container on init
tasks.context:
## Spec Context
AC-1: Single-file Astro component (consistent with ProjectShowcase.astro at 993 lines, CommandBar.astro at 617 lines).
AC-2: Career data from content/career-data.json loaded at build time in frontmatter.
AC-3: sigma.js + graphology imported client-side via <script> tag.
AC-4: Section retains id='skills' for goto compatibility.
AC-5: CSS uses only theme custom properties.
AC-6: Canvas for WebGL, CSS-styled overlays for tooltips.
AC-7: Graph state in plain JS.
AC-8: prefers-reduced-motion respected.

## Props Interface
```typescript
interface Props {
  skills: SkillCategory[];
  careerData: CareerData;
  revealIndex?: number;
}
```

## Node Visual Design
| Type | Palette Variable | Size |
|------|-----------------|------|
| role | --palette-4 | 12 (large) |
| company | --palette-6 | 10 (medium) |
| skill | --accent | 4-8 (by proficiency) |
| project | --palette-2 | 8 (medium) |
| education | --palette-3 | 8 (medium) |

## Edge Styles
- Default: 1px, --fg-muted at 0.3 opacity
- Highlighted: 2px, --accent at 1.0 opacity

## Tooltip
- Background: --bg-elevated, Border: --border
- Text: --fg-primary (label), --fg-secondary (details)
- Font: --font-mono for labels
- Max width: 280px

## Animation
- Stagger: companies (0ms) → roles (200ms) → education (400ms) → skills (600ms) → projects (800ms)
- Duration ~1.5s total with easeOutCubic
- prefers-reduced-motion: instant placement

## Theme Integration
- MutationObserver on document.documentElement data-theme attribute
- Re-read CSS custom properties on change
- Update node/edge colors in graphology → Sigma auto-re-renders

## Filter Integration
- Expose window.__graphFilter(keyword: string) globally
- Match against node label, category, type
- Highlight matches, dim non-matches
- Empty string resets

## Fallback
- .graph__fallback shows existing skills grid (rendered at build time)
- .graph__container hidden via style='display:none'
- On JS load: hide fallback, show container

## Key Patterns from Existing Components
Skills.astro: Section id='skills', .section-heading class, .skills__grid layout, .skills__tag items
ProjectShowcase.astro: data-reveal, data-reveal-index, skeleton patterns, modal pattern
CommandBar.astro: window.__terminal API pattern, theme event handling

## Accessibility
- role='img' with aria-label on graph container
- SR-only div with text list of all skills and connections
- Keyboard: Tab to graph region, zoom controls focusable
- ARIA labels on all buttons

IMPORTANT: The word 'blog' must NEVER appear. No Co-Authored-By in commits.

tasks.title: Update index.astro to use KnowledgeGraph component
tasks.description: Replace the Skills component import and usage in src/pages/index.astro with KnowledgeGraph. Import getCareerData from site.ts. Pass both skills (for fallback) and careerData to the component. Keep the same revealIndex (3).

verification.command: grep -c 'KnowledgeGraph' src/pages/index.astro && ! grep -q 'import Skills' src/pages/index.astro && echo 'OK'

tasks.title: Update CommandBar filter to support graph nodes
tasks.description: Modify the filter function in CommandBar.astro to call window.__graphFilter(keyword) when the function exists. Add the call after the existing skills/cards filtering logic. Also call window.__graphFilter('') on filter clear/reset.

tasks.title: Build verification and acceptance checks
tasks.description:
Run npx astro build to verify the project builds successfully. Check for:
1. Build completes without errors
2. No TypeScript errors
3. grep -r 'blog' src/ content/ returns 0 matches
4. KnowledgeGraph is referenced in index.astro
5. career-data.json has 60+ nodes
6. No 'Co-Authored-By' in recent commits
tasks.context:
## Verification Checklist
1. npx astro build — must pass
2. grep -ri 'blog' src/ content/ — must return 0 matches
3. grep -c 'KnowledgeGraph' src/pages/index.astro — must be >= 1
4. grep -c '__graphFilter' src/components/CommandBar.astro — must be >= 1
5. node -e "const d=require('./content/career-data.json'); if(d.nodes.length<60) process.exit(1);" — must pass
6. git log --oneline -10 | grep -i 'co-authored' — must return 0

If build fails, diagnose and fix the issue. Common problems:
- TypeScript type errors in the component
- Missing imports
- JSON syntax errors in career-data.json

IMPORTANT: No Co-Authored-By in commits.

tasks.title: Update CHANGELOG.md
tasks.description:
Add entries under [Unreleased] section for all changes in this feature. Use 'Added' and 'Changed' categories.

Added:
- Interactive career knowledge graph replacing flat skills grid
- WebGL graph rendering via Sigma.js + graphology
- Career data model with roles, companies, skills, projects, and education nodes
- ForceAtlas2 force-directed graph layout
- Animated node entrance with staggered type-based appearance
- Click-to-drill node highlighting with connected neighbor focus
- Hover tooltips with node details (label, type, description, dates)
- Drag-to-reposition, zoom/pan, and pinch-to-zoom touch support
- Graph zoom controls (zoom in, zoom out, reset)
- Node type legend overlay
- Screen reader accessible skills list fallback
- `window.__graphFilter()` API for command bar integration
- `prefers-reduced-motion` support for graph animations

Changed:
- Replaced Skills section with KnowledgeGraph component in index page
- Updated CommandBar filter to highlight/dim graph nodes via `__graphFilter`
- Added sigma, graphology, graphology-layout-forceatlas2 as dependencies
- Skills section heading changed to `## > career_knowledge_graph`
tasks.context:
